key_points: Off-campus luxury apartments are overpriced, poorly maintained, predatory
Skyvue residents report broken appliances, thin walls, flooding, false charges
Random roommate assignments can be very unpredictable
key_quotes: The WORST apartment building in every way imaginable. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!
CHARGED ME A 60 DOLLAR CLEANING FEE AND SENT ME TO COLLECTIONS WHICH FUCKED MY CREDIT ALL BECAUSE THEY WANTED TO CHARGE ME A CLEANING FEE FOR THE APARTMENT
My boyfriend lives here and it's been hell for him. Nearly every kitchen appliance has been broken at some point, the walls are so thin you can hear EVERYTHING

key_points: St. Patrick's Day drinking culture is intense; safety warnings circulate annually
Strong bar scene; Crunchy's ranked top-10 college bar nationally
Bus etiquette conflicts reveal tension between entitled and considerate students
key_quotes: I know you're all about to get absolutely wasted today, but ubers exist
Crunchy's made the list! From Iowa to New Orleans: The Athletic's top 10 college bars 2025
This is why i just walked everywhere lol

key_points: Beloved off-campus spots like No Thai close and leave lasting voids
Dairy Store ice cream is a genuine campus gem worth seeking out
Campus Spartys convenience stores sell questionable 225mg caffeine energy drinks
key_quotes: I miss No Thai so much. This is my last semester and im cursed to spend it without any no thai
I miss Dairy Store ice cream so much!
225mg caffeine it's too early in the semester to be drinking that shit

key_points: MSU cut 5 psychiatrists one month before fall semester started
CAPS told at least one student 'sorry we can't serve you and have no recommendations'
Mass shooting trauma still actively affects students years later
key_quotes: I got told by Caps last year 'sorry we can't serve you and have no recommendations' i literally was just looking to get my 5mg anxiety med refilled
I was so happy I lived because one of the classrooms that was attacked was one where I had a class eight hours earlier
I'm an instructor who works for free all summer because that's what the university implicitly demands of me

key_points: Michigan offers free community college; transfer route to MSU is well-trodden
Significant merit scholarships available; one poster received enough to choose MSU over Ivies
International students have limited aid options at this public university
key_quotes: I went to Michigan State University on a significant scholarship
CC is free in Michigan. My kid is going now, he is saving a lot of money, tiny classes, easy commute
I attended community college at Oakland Community College for two years, got my GPA up to 3.3, applied to MSU and was accepted

key_points: Some online async courses are widely criticized as a waste of tuition money
Professors rewarded for research, not teaching; affects classroom quality
Strong individual professors exist but course quality is highly inconsistent
key_quotes: If I just paid the $100 for the book instead of taking the class, I would learn just as much
Academia does not reward professors for good teaching; professors get promoted based on their research output
I took GBL323 in 2021. He authored a textbook that then had to be bought online. Fuck that, we pay for D2L support

key_points: Six presidents in six years; current Board of Trustees is widely seen as dysfunctional
Departing president publicly blasted trustees; called situation a national embarrassment
Budget crisis led to layoffs and department cuts while giving president a $1M raise
key_quotes: Lmao fuck this guy and fuck the Board. Glad MSU is so big and has so much going for it because not many schools could survive a decade of instability
Mid budget crisis and layoffs. Cool. Fuck the whole board of trustees
Wtf? What happened to the big budget crisis that necessitated a 9% cut across the entire university?

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful, especially in snow and fall colors
Cold Michigan winters are brutal; car ice and road salt are real concerns
East Lansing area has safety incidents including shootings and fires near campus
key_quotes: Not even funny how nice this campus is during the summer. Sucks that most of the regular year is cold lol
What in the fuck is up with this area? Two shootings, dead bodies being found in Arbor Glen and now a whole ass complex catching on fire?
I graduated 26 years ago and I miss this campus so much

key_points: MSU grads reach top positions in law, government, and entertainment
Strong accounting, engineering, and turf science programs with notable alumni
School brand opens doors; prestige matters less than performance here
key_quotes: I went to Michigan State and graduated near the top of my accounting class. It was a fantastic experience. I then went to University of Michigan for my MBA
Getting rejected from those top schools was the best thing that ever happened to me
Coraline producer Bill Mechanic has an English degree from MSU. Lots of Spartans in Hollywood

key_points: Athletics gets a $401M donation while students pay $170/semester just to use the gym
Parking fees, mandatory union dues, and poor WiFi frustrate students and staff
Grad students reportedly pressured to work unpaid summers, which is illegal
key_quotes: They still gonna charge students $170 a semester to workout at IM west??
Hey why dont you guys pay 50 bucks a month for parking? Maybe toss in another 50 for a useless union that you never see or hear from?
I heard of a lab that has told their grad students they will have to work this summer without pay

key_points: Strong Spartan pride and community loyalty survive constant institutional dysfunction
Warm, resilient student body that rallies after tragedies and adversity
Fun, large campus energy but awareness of real safety and admin problems
key_quotes: God I love it here
I love MSU but my god all the weird shit that happens here is just too funny. How much more can we take?
Our son graduated from MSU and never once regretted his decision to go there

key_points: Safety: campus shooting trauma ongoing; students report lasting PTSD symptoms
Administration: six presidents in six years; trustees publicly called dysfunctional by departing president
Mental Health: psychiatry unit gutted mid-crisis; CAPS turned students away without referrals
key_quotes: I hid in my dorm alone, in the dark, trying to fit under my roommate's bed and cover myself
We are a national embarrassment. The Governor needs to step up
I was a big part of the merger between counseling and psychiatry back when CAPS was first established. What in the world? They're getting rid of psychiatry entirely?

key_points: MSU Dairy Store ice cream is a beloved campus institution worth the trip
Dodgeball team is legitimately a national dynasty; five straight titles
Red Cedar River skating tradition is a unique and memorable campus experience
key_quotes: The river skating tradition at MSU is one of my favorite things about my alma mater. I only got to do it once, but it was a fantastic once
MSU Dodgeball Mixed wins the Michigan Dodgeball Cup for the fifth consecutive year
I've bragged to so many people that we've grown the grass for the FIFA World Cup. I used to joke that if I could go back, I'd have majored in turf management
